sourcePrompt: A coopered wooden barrel standing upright, about ninety centimeters tall. Vertical oak staves swell to a proud belly at the middle and taper to flat round ends, bound by four dark iron hoops - two near the ends, two flanking the bulge - with a small bung hole in one stave. Weathered brown oak and rust-spotted iron, sturdy and timeless.
handEdit: 16 thin stave-groove CSG blades removed (boolean blowup left the wall see-through with radial fins); flat-shaded 32-seg lathe facets read as staves; bung-hole diff kept
